sources.population: Patients receiving planned day surgery or procedure services in DoH-licensed facilities, with age, risk, procedure, anesthesia, and discharge qualifications.
sources.clinical_setting: DoH-licensed day surgery and procedure facilities in Abu Dhabi.
sources.applicability_note: UAE-direct for service governance, assessment, care plans, pre/intra/post-procedure documentation, records, discharge, and patient education. It does not replace anesthesiologist judgment, facility policy, consent, credentialing, or emergency care.

exact_sections.heading: Scope, definitions, and service requirements
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines the licensed day-surgery and procedure service, eligible service scope, and facility responsibilities.

exact_sections.heading: Governance, workforce, policies, and risk controls
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting governance, credentialing, safety, escalation, infection-control, medication, emergency, and quality policy context without generating a clinical action.

exact_sections.heading: Patient assessment and individualized care plan
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting pre-procedure assessment, history, examination, risk, suitability, investigations, consent, and an individualized clinician-entered care plan.

exact_sections.heading: Pre-procedure requirements
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting identity, procedure, site, consent, fasting, preparation, medicines, allergy, and pre-procedure checks when assessed or completed.

exact_sections.heading: Intra-procedure requirements
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports procedure, monitoring, equipment, staff, events, and clinical-record documentation during a procedure without directing treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Recovery and discharge requirements
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recovery observation, pain or nausea assessment, discharge criteria, instructions, escort, complications, handover, and follow-up only as clinician-completed documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Health-record requirements
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports complete, contemporaneous records for assessment, consent, anesthesia or sedation, procedure, monitoring, recovery, discharge, complications, and follow-up.

exact_sections.heading: Patient education and communication
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports patient-centred explanation, understandable language, interpreter support, written information, questions, and documented education.

sources.applicability_note: Exact for selected suspicious skin-lesion, safety-netting, and age- or risk-qualified respiratory recommendations; international suspected-cancer pathways require UAE clinician review. This batch also reviews recommendation-qualified upper-GI dysphagia, jaundice, and colorectal rectal-bleeding fields; no cancer diagnosis or automatic referral text is generated. exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.7.1–1.7.7 — skin cancers
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists suspicious pigmented-lesion features including size change, irregular shape or colour, diameter, inflammation, oozing and sensation change, plus dermoscopy and referral contexts for melanoma, squamous-cell carcinoma, and basal-cell carcinoma.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.14.6–1.15.2 — information and safety netting
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting warning symptoms, when to return for re-evaluation, agreed review, and patient-initiated review if symptoms recur, persist, worsen, or cause concern.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.6 — lung and pleural cancer respiratory features
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician recognition of unexplained haemoptysis and age- and smoking-context combinations involving unexplained cough, fatigue, breathlessness, chest pain, weight loss, appetite loss, recurrent chest infection, clubbing, lymphadenopathy, or chest signs.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.7 — oesophageal, stomach, and pancreatic cancer features
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician recognition of dysphagia, weight-loss combinations, and jaundice in recommendation-specific age and symptom contexts; it does not assert cancer or an automatic referral decision.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.5 — colorectal cancer features
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician recognition of rectal bleeding, change in bowel habit, abdominal pain, weight loss, anaemia, and rectal-mass contexts with recommendation-specific age qualifiers; it does not assert cancer.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.8.1–1.8.5 — laryngeal, oral, and thyroid cancer recognition features
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician recognition of recommendation-qualified persistent unexplained hoarseness, unexplained or persistent neck lump, oral ulceration lasting more than 3 weeks, lip or oral-cavity lump, red or red-and-white oral patch, and unexplained thyroid lump. It does not assert cancer or generate referral.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.4.1–1.4.3 — breast cancer recognition and referral
exact_sections.locator: recommendations organised by site, breast cancer section
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician recognition of an unexplained breast lump and age-qualified nipple or skin changes for referral consideration; it does not assert cancer or generate a referral decision.
